title: [Feature]: Network Bandwidth Limiter
body:
### Feature request description

Bandwidth limiter would allow limiting network traffic for specific pod or sets of pods. Something like [Cilium Bandwidth Manager](https://docs.cilium.io/en/v1.12/gettingstarted/bandwidth-manager/) would be awesome feature to have. It would allow providing better QoS.

### Suggest potential solution

_No response_

### Have you considered any alternatives?

I have considered using parent cgroup for a pod. An `iptables` filter would catch all outgoing packets from that cgroup and mark them. Then `tc` would apply bandwidth limit based on mark assigned by `iptables`. Haven't had much luck trying to make this plan live though.

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
to me, sounds like a netavark plugin.

I'd love if someone looked whether https://www.cni.dev/plugins/current/meta/bandwidth/ could be used. I'm not too familiar with Podman networking. Looks like this could be possibly applied fairly easy as it's CNI plugin and as far as I know Podman can use those.
@CDFN you could look at that CNI plugin and then write a netavark plugin -> https://github.com/containers/netavark/blob/main/plugin-API.md

Hello @baude,

I wanted to limit bandwidth usage of a single rootless podman container. I went through the links above but I wasn't able to understand where exactly the plugin has to be created and how to specify the plugin to the `podman run` command. I am not very well aquainted with the networking aspects of the rootless podman.

Could you please help me here by providing some example for the above use-case? That would be very helpful for me.

Thanks,
Tarun

@tarun360 Please file issues on netavark if the docs are not good enough.

As for this issue I am going to close this as I think it is to complicated for us to maintain.
